reasoning: Without search tools available, I must rely on base rates. Achieving 14 straight weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200 is extremely rare, accomplished by a tiny fraction of albums in history (e.g., Morgan Wallen's 'One Thing At A Time', Taylor Swift's 'The Tortured Poets Department'). Even for highly anticipated albums by mega-stars, predicting a block of 14 weeks without any competing major release knocking it off the top spot is highly unlikely. Given this low base rate, the probability of this specific album ('The Life of a Showgirl') doing so is incredibly low, pushing the estimate near the minimum limit.
